item0.description: Compare manual washing crews and downtime against automated dry-cleaning cycles scaled to your DC capacity.

howItWorks.heading: How to use this tool
howItWorks.subheading: Three steps from rough estimate to a conversation with our applications team.

step0.title: Enter plant inputs
step0.description: Ground-mount or rooftop, fixed tilt / seasonal tilt / trackers, automation level, MW or kW capacity, tariff, and module wattage.

step1.title: Run the estimate
step1.description: The model applies Taypro deployment assumptions, cycle cadence, dual-pass dry cleaning, and typical tariffs for your region (auto-selected from locale and location).

step2.title: Download or talk to us
step2.description: Export a PDF summary for internal review, then share it with our applications team for a plant-specific quote and SLA draft.

models.heading: Match results to the right robot
models.subheading: The calculator's automation and installation inputs align with Taypro's product line. Explore specifications before you finalize procurement.

robot0.description: Fully autonomous, waterless dry cleaning with dual-pass airflow and microfiber, AI scheduling for fixed and seasonal-tilt utility plants.

robot1.description: Portable pick-and-place dry cleaning robot for scattered blocks and plants that need flexible crew-assisted coverage.

robot2.description: Autonomous waterless robot for single-axis trackers, flexible bridge and 360° rotation across tracker brands.

robot3.description: Pay-per-panel-cleaned Opex, Taypro operates the cleaning robot fleet on your plant with no upfront CAPEX.

methodology.heading: What goes into the numbers
methodology.paragraph0: The calculator converts plant size, per-kWh tariff, assumed soiling recovery, and representative cleaning-cycle economics into annual generation gain, water savings, labour avoided, and a payback estimate. For India it reflects utility-scale conditions where Taypro robots operate today; for other regions it uses indicative local tariffs, labour, water, and yield, fixed-tilt or single-axis tracker sites with roughly 3–10 dry cleaning cycles per month.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.subheading: Common questions about robot pricing, payback, and how this tool relates to a formal Taypro proposal.
faq.q0: Is this solar panel cleaning robot price calculator the same as a formal quote?
faq.a0: No. This tool gives a directional ROI and investment band based on typical Taypro deployments. A formal quote accounts for your exact row lengths, tracker brand, soiling study, procurement model (CAPEX vs Taypro Opex), and service scope. Use the results as a starting point, then contact Taypro or open the lead form with your plant details.
faq.q1: What plant sizes does the ROI calculator support?
faq.a1: Ground-mount utility plants from 1 MW to 10 GW (model cap) and rooftop sites from 100 kW to 10 MW. Default tariffs follow your region, typically ~₹3/kWh ground and ~₹10/kWh rooftop in India, with local equivalents abroad (USD, AED, JPY, BDT, etc.). You can override any value. For very large sites, our team refines robot counts and pricing after layout review.
faq.q2: Which Taypro robot models does the calculator assume?
faq.a2: Automation level maps to fully automatic (GLYDE / GLYDE-X dual-pass or NYUMA / NYUMA-X PBT single-pass) versus semi-automatic (HELYX). Installation type adjusts multipliers for fixed tilt, seasonal tilt, or single-axis trackers. For specifications, see GLYDE, GLYDE-X, NYUMA, NYUMA-X, and HELYX product pages linked below.
faq.q3: Can I use Taypro Opex instead of buying robots outright?
faq.a3: Yes. Many developers choose Taypro Opex, pay per panel cleaned with Taypro operating the fleet. The calculator models CAPEX-style investment; for Opex economics, mention your preference when requesting a tailored quote and we will align the business case to your procurement model.
faq.q4: How accurate are the water and carbon figures?
faq.a4: Water savings assume dry cleaning replaces a representative wet-wash schedule. Carbon figures use standard grid emission factors applied to recovered generation. Both are indicative; your site's actual numbers depend on cleaning frequency, soiling rate, and regional grid intensity.
faq.q5: What does solar panel cleaning cost in India?
faq.a5: Cleaning cost depends on plant size, soiling, cycle count, and whether you buy robots (CAPEX) or use Taypro Opex (pay per panel cleaned). This calculator gives a directional investment and savings band; for a formal solar panel cleaning price or service quote, contact Taypro with your MW and layout.
faq.q6: Should I compare CAPEX robot purchase with Taypro Opex?
faq.a6: Yes. CAPEX suits owners who want to capitalise robots on the balance sheet; Taypro Opex moves cleaning to a monthly service line tied to panels cleaned. Use the automation toggle here for capex-style ROI, then read our solar panel cleaning service page for Opex economics.
